title: Bazaar Closures Reported in Tabriz and Isfahan
detail: Merchants in Tabriz Grand Bazaar and Isfahan's Qeysarieh Bazaar staged spontaneous closures to protest the rial's continued decline. Shop owners cited the inability to price goods when the currency lost 3-5% daily. Provincial governors urged calm, promising government intervention.

title: Official Inflation Reaches 52%
detail: Iran's Statistical Center reported annual inflation at 52.3%, with food prices up 72% year-over-year. Meat and dairy had become unaffordable for much of the population. Independent economists estimated real inflation closer to 70%, as the official basket underweighted essential goods.

title: Central Bank Stabilization Package Fails to Halt Slide
detail: The Central Bank announced a package of measures including increased supply of foreign currency at the NIMA exchange and restrictions on gold coin purchases. Within hours, the rial fell another 2% on the open market. Analysts noted the measures addressed symptoms rather than the structural sanctions-driven crisis.
